summarization.a: The figure displays three spectra charts labeled a, b, and c, each showing intensity against binding energy in electron volts at different stages in the ALE process.

data_extraction.a:
| Binding Energy (eV) | Intensity (a.u.) a |Intensity (a.u.) a |Intensity (a.u.) a |
|---|---|---|---|
| 470 | -|-|-|
| 465 | Peak | Peak | Peak |
| 459 | Ti-O | Ti-O | Ti-O |
| 456 | Ti-N | Ti-N | Ti-N |
|450|-|-|-|

a.question: To what bond is the surface modification mostly selective?
a.answer: a	The red spectrum shows that especially the Ti-N peak is reduced after modification, so the step is selective for Ti-N and not for Ti-O.

a.question: Is the spectrum taken at the surface or in the bulk?
a.answer: a	The measurement is likely performed at the surface as there is quite some oxygen present, whereas in the bulk it should be lower.

a.question: At what energy is the Ti-O bond active?
a.answer: a	The bond is active at 459 eV.

a.question: How would the Ti-N be described with respect to the oxygen peak?
a.answer: a	The peak can be seen as a shoulder of the oxygen peak.
